mission.title: lima: Stuck network when using multiple connections with k3s
mission.description: Stuck network when using multiple connections with k3s. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify lima troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your lima installation:
```bash
limactl --version
limactl list
```
Look for errors or warnings that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review lima configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant lima configuration:
When running a non-trivial web app in a Lima VM using the `k3s` template, the network stops being responsive. This seems to happen when multiple persistent HTTP connections are established.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Stuck network when using multiple connections with k3s
steps.description:
I had some problems initially to reproduce it; I was opening lots of tabs with `http://localhost:31833` quickly, and they all worked. But when I waited a bit between opening tabs, I would eventually see the failure.

The corresponding error in the hostagent log is:

```json
{"error":"close tcp 127.0.0.1:6443-\u003e127.0.0.1:55606: shutdown: socket is not connected","level":"debug","msg":"failed to call CloseRead","time":"2025-06-03T10:18:56-07:00"}
```

It seems to be a problem with the GRPC

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm Stuck network when using multiple connections… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test lima to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: I had some problems initially to reproduce it; I was opening lots of tabs with `http://localhost:31833` quickly, and they all worked. But when I waited a bit between opening tabs, I would eventually see the failure.

prerequisites.description: A working lima installation or development environment.
